title: payena obscura var. havilandii
intro: Payena obscura var. havilandii, also known as the Haviland's payena, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Payena obscura var. havilandii include the potential treatment of various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by boiling the roots or leaves in water to create a decoction, which can be used to treat various health issues.
